title: The Human Face
description: Who the reskinned-monster villain appears to be — the ordinary, trusted, or admired human form that hides a monster's stat block.
entries: a fallen archmage in ash-grey robes, soft-spoken and tired
a beloved war-hero whose smile never quite reaches the eyes
a plague-doctor whose beaked mask has not come off in years
a syndicate spymaster to whom half the city owes a favour
a grand inquisitor with a saint's spotless reputation
a merchant-prince who buys cities the way others buy bread
a court physician trusted with every royal secret
a wandering monk who asks only to be left in peace
a widowed noble in perpetual, immaculate mourning
a celebrated duelist who has never once been beaten
the kindly headmaster of a well-loved orphanage
a disgraced general selling their sword to the highest bidder
a serene oracle the desperate travel miles to consult
a charming diplomat with a ready answer for everything
a scarred hermit the village children dare each other to visit
a rising young senator with impeccable manners and no past
